     The Numbers Business Report

The Descendants (2011)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $82,624,961Details
International Box Office $92,882,839
Worldwide Box Office $175,507,800
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $17,076,245
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $5,271,132
Total $22,347,377
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Matt King is an indifferent husband and father of two girls, who is forced to re-examine his past and embrace his future when his wife suffers a boating accident off of Waikiki. The event leads to a rapprochement with his young daughters while Matt wrestles with a decision to sell the family’s land handed down from Hawaiian royalty and missionaries.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$1,190,096 (1.4% of total gross)
Legs:11.25 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:47.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$20,000,000 (worldwide box office is 8.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:29 opening theaters/2,038 max. theaters, 7.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $124,374,204

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 16th, 2011 (Limited) by Fox Searchlight
December 9th, 2011 (Expands Wide) by Fox Searchlight
International Releases: January 26th, 2012 (Wide) (Bahrain)
January 26th, 2012 (Wide) (New Zealand)
February 16th, 2012 (Wide) (Denmark)
February 24th, 2012 (Wide) (Lithuania)
March 8th, 2012 (Wide) (Serbia and Montenegro)
Video Release: March 13th, 2012 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for language including some sexual references.
(Rating bulletin 2160, 2/23/2011)
Running Time: 110 minutes
Award nomination: 2012 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: Golden Globe Winner Best Picture—Drama, Writers Guild of America Best Adapted Screenplay Winner
Cast, crew, or production detail: Character's Journey Shown on Map, Voiceover/Narration
Plot point: Coming of Age, Death of a Son or Daughter, Death of a Spouse or Fiancée / Fiancé, Dysfunctional Family, Infidelity, Inheritance, Land Developer, Medical and Hospitals, Surprise Twist, Young Child Dealing with the Death of a Parent
Source material: Inspired by a True Story
Subgenre: Comedy Drama
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Fox Searchlight Pictures, Ad Hominem Enterpises, Dune Entertainment, Little Blair Productions, Ingenious Film Partners
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

Matt King is an indifferent husband and father of two girls, who is forced to re-examine his past and embrace his future when his wife suffers a boating accident off of Waikiki. The event leads to a rapprochement with his young daughters while Matt wrestles with a decision to sell the family’s land handed down from Hawaiian royalty and missionaries.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$1,190,096 (1.4% of total gross)
Legs:11.25 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:47.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$20,000,000 (worldwide box office is 8.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:29 opening theaters/2,038 max. theaters, 7.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $124,374,204

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 16th, 2011 (Limited) by Fox Searchlight
December 9th, 2011 (Expands Wide) by Fox Searchlight
International Releases: January 26th, 2012 (Wide) (Bahrain)
January 26th, 2012 (Wide) (New Zealand)
February 16th, 2012 (Wide) (Denmark)
February 24th, 2012 (Wide) (Lithuania)
March 8th, 2012 (Wide) (Serbia and Montenegro)
Video Release: March 13th, 2012 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for language including some sexual references.
(Rating bulletin 2160, 2/23/2011)
Running Time: 110 minutes
Award nomination: 2012 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: Golden Globe Winner Best Picture—Drama, Writers Guild of America Best Adapted Screenplay Winner
Cast, crew, or production detail: Character's Journey Shown on Map, Voiceover/Narration
Plot point: Coming of Age, Death of a Son or Daughter, Death of a Spouse or Fiancée / Fiancé, Dysfunctional Family, Infidelity, Inheritance, Land Developer, Medical and Hospitals, Surprise Twist, Young Child Dealing with the Death of a Parent
Source material: Inspired by a True Story
Subgenre: Comedy Drama
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Fox Searchlight Pictures, Ad Hominem Enterpises, Dune Entertainment, Little Blair Productions, Ingenious Film Partners
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
